Reduction API

Reduction Recipes

EchelleReduce.ReduceRecipe — Type
ReduceRecipe

Base type for reduction recipes.

source

Primary reduction methods

EchelleReduce.reduce — Function
reduce(recipe::ReduceRecipe)

Perform reduction steps according to the recipe. The default steps are:

  1. create_output_dirs(recipe)
  2. data = initialize_data(recipe)
  3. gen_master_calib_images(recipe, data)
  4. traces, sregions = get_traces(recipe, data)
  5. extract(recipe, data, traces, sregions)
source
EchelleReduce.initialize_data — Function
initialize_data

Method intended to initialize parse the input directory and categorize the data. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.create_output_dirs — Function
create_output_dirs

Method intended to create any output directories. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.gen_master_calib_images — Function
gen_master_calib_images

Method intended to generate all calibration frames. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.get_master_flat — Function
get_master_flat

Method intended to get the appropriate master flat frame for calibration. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.get_master_dark — Function
get_master_dark

Method intended to get the appropriate master dark frame for calibration. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.get_traces — Function
get_traces

Method intended to get the appropriate vector of trace params. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.get_trace_spacing — Function

gettracespacing Returns the minimum trace spacing.

source
EchelleReduce.get_trace_height — Function

gettraceheight Returns the maximum trace height.

source
EchelleReduce.get_specregion2d — Function
get_specregion2d

Returns the appropriate SpecRegion2d

source
EchelleReduce.get_extract_orders — Function
get_extract_orders

Gets the orders to be extracted. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.get_read_noise — Function
get_read_noise(itime::Real, dark_current::Real=0, read_noise::Real=0)
get_read_noise(data::SpecData, dark_current::Real=0, read_noise::Real=0)

Compute the effective read noise in units of photoelectrons.

source
EchelleReduce.extract — Function
extract

Method intended to perform all necessary extraction steps. Must be implemented.

source

Pre-extraction Calibration

EchelleReduce.PreCalib.gen_master_dark — Function
gen_master_dark(master_dark::MasterCal2d; master_bias=nothing)

Generate the master dark image.

source
EchelleReduce.PreCalib.gen_master_flat — Function
gen_master_flat(master_flat::MasterCal2d; master_bias=nothing, master_dark=nothing, p=0.5)

Generate the master flat field image, normalized to p.

source
EchelleReduce.PreCalib.pre_calibrate! — Function
pre_calibrate!(image::AbstractMatrix; master_dark=nothing, master_flat=nothing)

Pre-calibrate an image according to the master dark and flat.

source
EchelleReduce.PreCalib.gen_master_coadded_image — Function
gen_master_coadded_image(master_frame::SpecData2d)

Co-adds frames.

source

Order Tracing

EchelleReduce.Tracing.trace — Function
trace(data::SpecData2d, sregion::SpecRegion2d; trace_pos_deg=2, min_order_spacing::Int, xleft=nothing, xright=nothing, n_slices=20, fiber=nothing)

Util peaks within a given vector together

  • data The data
  • traces The vector of trace parameters (dictionaries)
  • ny. The number of vertical pixels.
  • nx. The number of horizontal pixels.
  • sregion. The spectral region to further bound the left and right ends of the image.
source
trace(data::SpecData2d, sregion::SpecRegion2d; trace_pos_deg=2, min_order_spacing::Int, xleft=nothing, xright=nothing, n_slices=20, fiber=nothing)

Util peaks within a given vector together

  • data The data
  • data_image
  • traces The vector of trace parameters (dictionaries)
  • ny. The number of vertical pixels.
  • nx. The number of horizontal pixels.
  • sregion. The spectral region to further bound the left and right ends of the image.
source
EchelleReduce.Tracing.gen_trace_image — Function
gen_trace_image(traces::Vector, ny, nx, sregion::SpecRegion2d)

Generate an nx x ny image with values according to the labels in each trace dictionary.

  • traces The vector of trace parameters (dictionaries)
  • ny. The number of vertical pixels.
  • nx. The number of horizontal pixels.
  • sregion. The spectral region to further bound the left and right ends of the image.
source

Spectral Extraction

EchelleReduce.Extract.SpectralExtractor — Type
SpectralExtractor

The base type for a spectral extraction algorithm.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.extract_image — Function
extract_image(extractor::SpectralExtractor, data::SpecData2d, data_image::AbstractMatrix, sregion::SpecRegion2d, traces::Vector; badpix_mask::Union{AbstractMatrix, Nothing}=nothing, read_noise::Real=0)

Primary method to extract all orders from an image.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.extract_trace — Function
extract_trace

Primary method to extract a single trace. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.gen_model2d — Function
extract_trace

Primary method to generate a 2d model. Must be implemented.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.plot_extracted_spectrum — Function
plot_extracted_spectrum(recipe::ReduceRecipe, data::SpecData2d, reduced_data::Vector, fname::String, traces::Vector)

Plots all reduced_data corresponding to traces extracted from a full-frame image and saves the image to fname.

source

Spectral Extraction Utility Methods

EchelleReduce.Extract.estimate_snr — Function
estimate_snr(trace_image::AbstractMatrix)

Crude method to estimate the mean S/N.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.compute_trace_positions_centroids — Function
compute_trace_positions_centroids(trace_image::AbstractMatrix, trace_mask::AbstractMatrix, sregion::SpecRegion2d, trace_positions::Polynomial, aperture; trace_pos_poly_deg=nothing)

Compute the trace positions by fitting a polynomial to the column centroids.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.refine_trace_window — Function
refine_trace_window(image::AbstractMatrix, badpix_mask::AbstractMatrix, sregion::SpecRegion2d, trace_positions_estimate::Polynomial; trace_pos_poly_deg=nothing, window::Vector, n_iterations=3)

Iteratively refines the initial trace window using the centroids of each column.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.compute_background_1d — Function
compute_background_1d(trace_image::AbstractMatrix, trace_mask::AbstractMatrix, trace_positions::Polynomial, extract_aperture; smooth_width=nothing)

Estimates the background signal by considering regions of low flux according to the provided extract_aperture.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.flag_pixels2d! — Function
flag_pixels2d!(trace_image::AbstractMatrix, trace_mask::AbstractMatrix, model2d::AbstractMatrix, nσ::Real)

Flags bad pixels in the 2d image by looking at outliers in the residuals.

source
EchelleReduce.Extract.fix_bad_pixels_interp — Function
fix_bad_pixels_interp(trace_image::AbstractMatrix, xmin, xmax, poly_bottom::Polynomial, poly_top::Polynomial)

Utility function to fix bad pixels in an image with linear interpolation.

source

Optimal Spectral Extraction

EchelleReduce.OptimalExtraction.EmpiricalOptimalExtractor — Type
EmpiricalOptimalExtractor(;max_iterations=20, remove_background=false, background_smooth_width=0, oversample_profile=8, trace_pos_poly_deg=4, badpix_σ=6, extract_aperture=:auto)

Construct an EmpiricalOptimalExtractor object with the following parameters.

  • max_iterations: The maximum number of iterations to run. In each iteration, the trace positions, profile, and bad pixel variables are updated. Convergence is reached when no more bad pixels are found.
  • remove_background: Whether or not to estimate and remove a background
  • background_smooth_width: The rolling window size to smooth the background with. Ignored if remove_background is false.
  • oversample_profile: Integer oversample factor for the trace profile cubic spline.
  • trace_pos_poly_deg: Polynomial degree for the trace positions, determined from a polynomial fit to the centroids of detector columns.
  • badpix_σ: Deviations larger than badpix_σ are flagged after extraction.
  • extract_aperture: The aperture window to consider in extraction. Note that the background will only utilize pixels outside this window. Alternatively, :auto can be passed to automatically determine an optimal window from the profile.
source
EchelleReduce.OptimalExtraction.optimal_extraction — Function
optimal_extraction(trace_image::AbstractMatrix, trace_mask::AbstractMatrix, trace_positions::Polynomials.Polynomial, trace_profile, extract_aperture, background=nothing, background_err=nothing, read_noise=0, max_iterations=20)

Perform optimal extraction as described in Horne et al. 1985.

source
